title: Diagnose an iOS memory leak
description: Use Instruments to find leaked objects, trace the retain cycle, and break it with a weak/unowned capture.
body: Steps: 1. Reproduce growth: push and pop a screen repeatedly and watch memory climb. 2. Run the Leaks/Allocations instrument and confirm view controllers or objects are not deallocating. 3. Inspect the retain graph to find the cycle (often a closure, timer, delegate, or Combine subscription holding self strongly). 4. Break it: [weak self] in the escaping closure, weak delegate, or cancel the subscription. 5. Re-run and confirm the objects deallocate on dismiss. Rollback point: before changing capture semantics broadly (unowned can crash if wrong). Validation: Instruments shows the controller deallocating and steady memory across navigation; a leak that 'goes away' only because you stopped testing is not fixed.
